title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

The 2019 world map displays Physical Integrity Rights Scores on a continuous scale ranging from -4 to 4, where the legend transitions from dark red for the lowest scores to dark blue for the highest. Scores are based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources. In the Americas, Canada and Greenland appear in the highest blue bands, indicating scores between 3 and 4, while the United States sits in the lightest orange band near the 0 to -1 range. Mexico and most of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, cluster in the light orange bands between 0 and -2. Europe shows a gradient: Nordic countries like Norway and Sweden are in the darker blue bands (3 to 4), Western European nations generally occupy the light blue bands (1 to 2), and Eastern European countries vary between light blue and light orange. Across Africa, the distribution is mixed; South Africa and Namibia appear in the light blue bands (1 to 2), while a band of countries across the Sahel and North Africa, including Algeria and Sudan, sit in the darker orange bands (-2 to -3). In Asia, India and China are positioned in the light orange bands near 0 to -1, whereas Myanmar and Yemen appear in the darkest red bands, indicating scores between -3 and -4. Australia and New Zealand are colored in the lightest blue, corresponding to scores between 1 and 2. Greenland is marked with hatching, indicating no data is available for that region.
